2604.12647.title: Adaptive Test-Time Scaling for Zero-Shot Respiratory Audio Classification
2604.12647.paper_summary: Automated respiratory audio analysis promises scalable, non-invasive disease screening, yet progress is limited by scarce labeled data and costly expert annotation. Zero-shot inference eliminates task-specific supervision, but existing methods apply uniform computation to every input regardless of difficulty. We introduce TRIAGE, a tiered zero-shot framework that adaptively scales test-time compute by routing each audio sample through progressively richer reasoning stages: fast label-cosine scoring in a joint audio-text embedding space (Tier-L), structured matching with clinician-style descriptors (Tier-M), and retrieval-augmented large language model reasoning (Tier-H). A confidence-based router finalizes easy predictions early while allocating additional computation to ambiguous inputs, enabling nearly half of all samples to exit at the cheapest tier. Across nine respiratory classification tasks without task-specific training, TRIAGE achieves a mean AUROC of 0.744, outperforming prior zero-shot methods and matching or exceeding supervised baselines on multiple tasks. Our analysis show that test-time scaling concentrates gains where they matter: uncertain cases see up to 19% relative improvement while confident predictions remain unchanged at minimal cost.
2604.12647.paper_summary_zh: 自動化呼吸音頻分析承諾可擴展的非侵入性疾病篩檢，但進展受到稀缺標記數據和昂貴專家註釋的限制。零-shot推斷消除了特定任務的監督，但現有方法對每個輸入應用統一計算，無論其難度如何。我們介紹了TRIAGE，一個分層的零-shot框架，通過將每個音頻樣本路由到逐步豐富的推理階段來自適應地擴展測試時間的計算：在聯合音頻-文本嵌入空間中的快速標籤餘弦得分（Tier-L），與臨床風格描述符的結構匹配（Tier-M），以及檢索增強的大型語言模型推理（Tier-H）。基於信心的路由器提前完成簡單預測，同時將額外計算分配給模糊輸入，使近一半的樣本能夠在最便宜的層級退出。在九個沒有特定任務訓練的呼吸分類任務中，TRIAGE實現了0.744的平均AUROC，超越了先前的零-shot方法，並在多個任務上與監督基準相匹配或超過。我們的分析顯示，測試時間的擴展將收益集中在重要的地方：不確定的案例相對改善高達19%，而自信的預測在最低成本下保持不變。
2604.12647.author: Tsai-Ning Wang et.al.
2604.12647.authors: Tsai-Ning Wang, Herman Teun den Dekker, Lin-Lin Chen, Neil Zeghidour, Aaqib Saeed
